description: Deke urgently informs Mack that the Zephyr has vanished, leaving them stuck in 1982. Mack is deeply frustrated and grief-stricken over his parents' deaths and shoes Deke away, expressing bitterness regarding the tampering with time. Deke attempts to comfort Mack by relating to his own losses, but Mack isolates himself further, leaving Deke to handle their predicament alone.

transcript.line: Mack! Mack! Mack, it's the Zephyr... it's gone. It jumped away. We're stranded.
Yeah, well, you got a whole lot of 1982 instead.
They left us behind. Something must have gone wrong.
Or the mission's over. Coulson's explosion should've taken out the Chronicoms. So, I guess we won.
Then why would we pit stop in ‘82 for all of five minutes? That doesn't make any sense.
None of this makes any damn sense! We can't understand it. That's why no one should ever mess with time, no matter how bad things get. The past... is sacred.
Listen, I'm sorry that you've lost your parents. If there's anything else we could have done differently...
You could have followed orders. Who are you to take a life into your own hands, even if you think it's right? Ripples, not waves, remember?
I lost my parents early, too. Okay? And I still think about it every day. If you want to talk, I'm here.
Wait. Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait. Wait, wait, wait. We... We need a game plan here. W-What if the Chronicoms are still out there? Maybe that's why we're here.
M-Mack! You don't have to go through this alone!
Alphie, Reuben, don't forget your bags.
Where the hell have you been? Your store has been closed all week. I want my computer back, nerd.
